original_abstract: The purpose of this article is to reveal the influence of the main areas of international educational integration on the processes of reforming the national professional education. The paper presents the main areas of the international educational integration at the level of statutory regulation, at the level of the goals and mission of higher professional education, at the level of organizational-structural changes, at the level of quality assurance of professional education, at the training content and management levels. The authors also proved the competitive advantages and disadvantages of the Russian market of educational services, the benefits and risks of reforming professional education under the conditions of international educational integration, economic and social effects of teaching foreign students in Russian universities. The article contents may be used by departments of international cooperation of professional educational institutions in the development of mechanisms for integrating Russian professional schools into a single educational space, as well as in the development of courses and teaching and learning aids for university heads on the issue of reforming professional education in the context of the Bologna and Copenhagen processes.
humanized_abstract: MAIN DIRECTIONS OF INTERNATIONAL EDUCATIONAL INTEGRATION AND THEIR IMPACT ON DEVELOPING THE NATIONAL SYSTEM OF PROFESSIONAL EDUCATION The article examines the impact of the main areas of international educational integration on the national system of professional education. The main areas of international educational integration that are considered in the article are: statutory regulation of educational activities at the international level, setting the priorities of higher professional education in line with international standards, optimizing the structure and staff of higher schools, integrating quality assurance systems in professional education, modernizing the content and management of educational activities. The competitive positions and advantages of the Russian educational services market, the impact of the reform of professional education on the adaptation of Russian higher schools to the conditions of international educational integration, as well as the economic and social efficiency of teaching foreign students in Russian universities are also considered. The article materials can be used by the International Cooperation Departments of professional educational institutions for the development of measures for the integration of Russian professional schools into a single educational space, as well as for the development of curricula, training and teaching aids for the retraining of university presidents on the issues of reforming professional education in the context of the Bologna and Copenhagen processes.
